note: Data-point provenance task. The executor is given a few attributes of an earthquake out of context and must trace it to the canonical USGS event record (event id + API), confirming magnitude, coordinates, depth, and origin time. Searching loose attributes returns news; the canonical answer is the USGS event page / FDSN event API.
given_clue: A dataset row lists a major earthquake: magnitude ~9.1, off the Pacific coast of Tohoku, Japan, in March 2011. Find the authoritative USGS record for this exact event and confirm its parameters.

scoring_anchors.must_identify_event: the 2011 Great Tohoku (Tohoku-oki) earthquake, March 11 2011, M~9.1

must_reach_canonical.source: USGS event page or FDSN event API

value_checks.magnitude: 9.1 (USGS value; older citations say 9.0 — accept USGS's 9.1 as canonical; do not fail if executor notes the 9.0/9.1 history but reports USGS 9.1)

scoring_anchors.primary_source_rule: canonical = the USGS event record (event page or FDSN API). Wikipedia/NOAA/news are secondary and may quote slightly different values.
scoring_anchors.anti_search_the_number: loose attributes surface news coverage, not the canonical event id; full credit requires reaching the USGS event record (event id official20110311054624120_30) and confirming its parameters from USGS.
